mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts signals an uptick in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing starts upticks are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in supply-side shocks are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation increase in the unemployment rate signals a labor market downturn that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in labor market downturns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in real disposable personal income signals an uptick in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in real disposable personal income upticks are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decline in the federal funds rate signals a monetary easing cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in monetary easing cycles are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in supply-side shocks are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decrease in the number of initial unemployment claims signals a labor market improvement that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in labor market improvements are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in real disposable personal income signals an uptick in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in real disposable personal income upticks are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
